kamloops.title: Firefighters battling wildfire near Lytton, B.C., helped by cooler temperatures
kamloops.description: The latest estimate puts the size of the out-of-control fire near Lytton, B.C., at about seven square-kilometres, up slightly from Saturday.

kamloops.title: Saw Creek wildfire grows to 600 hectares as Lytton remains under evacuation alert
kamloops.description: The out-of-control Saw Creek wildfire near Lytton has grown to 600 hectares as crews battle hot, dry conditions and residents face smoke and water advisories.

all.title: From forest to front door – understanding wildfire spread into communities
all.description: By Ty Burke As California’s population boomed — from 10 million in 1950 to over 40 million today — the number of people living in fire-prone areas multiplied. Over the decades, millions of new homes and commercial buildings sprang up to accommodate the needs of the state’s growing population, and many of those structures stand […]

all.title: Significant wildfire activity heading into Juneteenth weekend drives National Preparedness Level increase
all.description: The National Interagency Fire Center increased the National Preparedness Level to PL3 on Thursday, marking a shift toward needed national support as the U.S. nears peak fire season. The increase was driven by significant wildland fire activity across the country, with the potential of additional large fires remaining elevated in the days ahead, the NIFC […]

all.title: Linking wildfire risk to social vulnerability
all.description: Socially vulnerable groups are significantly over-represented in high wildfire-risk areas across the United States and should be prioritized for targeted mitigation measures, research has found. A study in the International Journal of Wildland Fire identified census tracts across the US where high wildfire risk and high social vulnerability intersect – and found that American Indian/Alaska [

all.title: Bringing wildfire scenario planning to life
all.description: As fire and other emergencies become more complex, chaotic and concurrent, incident managers may find that traditional pre-season tabletop exercises struggle to cope with these new demands. An emerging Australian company has seized the power of AI to develop a scenario package that moves faster, adapts to sudden changes, reacts to multiple pressures, and responds […]

all.title: Wildland firefighter health should be treated as fire infrastructure, urges emerging research focus
all.description: The physiological and psychological stress that wildfire personnel are put under every fire season has quickly moved from the margins of fire research to the mainstream, with global pathways for funding increasingly expanding, according to International Journal of Wildland Fire Associate Editor Anthony White. White, in an editorial in the Journal, said he has seen […]

all.title: Wildfire Prevention Act becomes ‘Trojan Horse’ for Roadless Rule repeal
all.description: Support for a once bipartisan piece of wildfire-focused legislation has faltered after Republican lawmakers added a repeal of the Roadless Rule. Energy and Natural Resources Committee Chairman Sen. Mike Lee (R-UT) proposed the amendment to the Wildfire Prevention Act, which would increase fuels treatments in federal forests and had support from both Democrats and Republicans […]

all.title: Marcos Aerospace Unveils the Quencher concept
all.description: This firefighting aircraft promises to do it all – amphibious, pilotless, day and night ops, fleet coordination and an AI brain. Marcos Aerospace, a Greek aerospace startup based in Athens, has just launched Quencher, an autonomous amphibious aircraft currently in what the company is describing as “the preliminary design phase.” With a 4,500-litre water capacity, […]

all.title: Elevated fire weather forecasted throughout the US this week
all.description: The “very active” fire season predicted for the U.S. since the beginning of the year may largely kick off this week, according to forecasts from the National Interagency Fire Center. Elevated fire weather conditions are set across the Southwest and southern Great Basin regions due to strong winds, dry conditions and very warm temperatures, according […]

all.title: US Wildland Fire Service partially funded from significant EPA cuts
all.description: It’s been nearly half a year since the U.S. Wildland Fire Service was formally launched, but the new agency has gone without funding since. Lawmakers may soon change that. The U.S. House Committee on Appropriations submitted its funding plan for the Department of the Interior, the environment and related agencies this week, with a specific […]

all.title: Europe preps for a wild wildfire summer
all.description: Europe has boosted its wildfire preparations ahead of a forecasted busy 2026 season by deploying its largest force yet of firefighters, aircraft and other experts across the continent. The announcement comes as the World Meteorological Organisation issued a Global Seasonal Climate Update that, while highlighting the development of a strong El Nino around the Pacific, […]
